channel.title: Naija Digest Online
channel.description: Nigeria’s one-stop hub for breaking news, tech, football, celebrity gossip, Naija music, live TV, exchange rates and trending topics.

item.title: BREAKING: ICPC accuses El-Rufai of breaching medical visit, arrests personal doctor
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The ICPC has accused former Kaduna State Governor, Nasir El-Rufai, of breaching his court-approved medical visit conditions and arrested his personal docto
Read More: https://punchng.com/breaking-icpc-accuses-el-rufai-of-breaching-medical-visit-arrests-personal-doctor/

item.title: 6.2m Nigerians face hunger in 2026, child rights organisation warns
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Save the Children International warns 36.2 million Nigerians face hunger in 2026 lean season due to conflict, climate shocks, and rising food prices. Urgen
Read More: https://punchng.com/6-2m-nigerians-face-hunger-in-2026-child-rights-organisation-warns/

item.title: APC first-term governors back Tinubu’s re-election bid
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
APC first-term governors unanimously endorse President Bola Tinubu for re-election, citing policy continuity and national reforms. Read their full statemen
Read More: https://punchng.com/apc-first-term-governors-back-tinubus-re-election-bid/

Three Nigerian students secured four gold medals at the International STEM Olympiad in Rome, showcasing Nigeria’s talent on the global stage. Learn more ab
Read More: https://punchng.com/three-nigerian-students-win-gold-at-international-stem-olympiad-in-rome/

item.title: Anambra approves new plan to tackle erosion, student housing deficit
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Anambra State Government approves new measures under “Solution 2.0” to tackle erosion, student housing deficit, and boost development. Learn more about the
Read More: https://punchng.com/anambra-approves-new-plan-to-tackle-erosion-student-housing-deficit/

item.title: Six arrested as mob kills two over false kidnapping allegation in Kaduna
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Six suspects have been arrested in Kaduna after a mob lynched two travellers over a false kidnapping allegation. Police condemn the jungle justice.
Read More: https://punchng.com/six-arrested-as-mob-kills-two-over-false-kidnapping-allegation-in-kaduna/

item.title: NDLEA seizes illicit drugs, arrests 42 suspects
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
The NDLEA Edo Command intercepted 4,905.36 kg of illicit drugs and arrested 42 suspects in June, destroying three cannabis farms in a major crackdown.
Read More: https://punchng.com/ndlea-seizes-illicit-drugs-arrests-42-suspects/

item.title: VIDEO: Why I dance to promote my movies — Funke Akindele
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Nollywood star Funke Akindele reveals why she uses dancing to promote her movies, citing financial hardship and the need for innovative marketing strategie
Read More: https://punchng.com/video-why-i-dance-to-promote-my-movies-funke-akindele/

item.title: Police arrest fake officer, recover uniforms in Akwa Ibom
item.dc:creator: Punch
item.description:
Akwa Ibom Police have arrested a fake police officer, Precious Joseph, recovering uniforms and other accoutrements used for impersonation and unlawful acti
Read More: https://punchng.com/police-arrest-fake-officer-recover-uniforms-in-akwa-ibom/

Read More: https://punchng.com/ex-gen-olukolade-backs-state-police-warns-against-delays-govs-abuse/

item.description: Latest score from the third T20 international of the five-match series between England and India at Trent Bridge. Watch on Sky Sports Cricket.

item.title: NABTEB releases examination results, 10,426 candidates score above 50%
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: The National Business and Technical Examinations Board, NABTEB, has released the results of the 2026 National Common Entrance Examination, NCEE, with 10,426 candidates, representing 75.29 per cent, scoring 50 per cent and above. DAILY POST reports that Dr Mohammed Aminu Mohammed, the Registrar and C

item.title: Plateau APC Primary: PKK Electoral Officers Allege Attempts To Induce Them To Discredit Poll

item.description: Officials who conducted the All Progressives Congress House of Representatives primary election for the Pankshin/Kanke/Kanam Federal Constituency in Plateau State have alleged attempts by some political actors to induce and intimidate them into discrediting the outcome of the exercise. The officials

item.title: APC first-term govs back Tinubu’s economic reforms, express concern over insecurity
item.dc:creator: Daily Post
item.description: Governors elected on the platform of the All Progressives Congress, APC, for their first term have backed the economic reforms of President Bola Tinubu while expressing concern over the country’s security challenges. The position was contained in a communiqué issued at the end of the APC first-term

item.title: Russian Embassy disputes reports on death of Nigerian student in Ukraine

item.description: In a statement issued on Tuesday, the embassy expressed its deepest condolences to the family and friends of the deceased over their loss. The post Russian Embassy disputes reports on death of Nigerian student in Ukraine appeared first on Vanguard News.

item.description: The Nigerian Bar Association (NBA) has rejected an alleged directive attributed to the Attorney General of the Federation (AGF)... The post NBA rejects alleged AGF directives, insists on 2026 national officers’ election appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Emerald HoldCo makes N6.9 billion takeover bid for Beta Glass’ minority shares
item.dc:creator: Premium Times
item.description: The core investor is tabling N590.94 to purchase each of the shares, which were quoted at N562.8 per unit at the start of trade on Lagos’ Customs Street on Tuesday, placing the offer at a 5 per cent premium above the market price. item.title: Intersex Nigerian woman gives birth to baby boy
item.dc:creator: PM News

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets

Oil nudged higher following attacks on shipping in and around the Strait of Hormuz, highlighting continu

item.title: FG to unlock agriculture potential in South-East
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: FG has launched a fertiliser support programme for farmers in the South-East to boost food production, jobs and agricultural development. The post FG to unlock agriculture potential in South-East appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.description: Growing concerns over Lassa fever infections in northern Nigeria have prompted the Institute of Human Virology Nigeria (IHVN) and the Abubakar Tafawa Balewa University Teaching Hospital (ATBUTH) to launch a major research project aimed at accelerating the development of a vaccine against the deadly

item.title: Suspected Bandits Kill Two Farmers In Sokoto Community
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Two farmers have been reportedly killed in a fresh attack by suspected bandits in Isa Local Government Area of Sokoto State. The attack sparked renewed concerns over the safety of farming communities as residents begin this year’s cultivation season. The victims were said to have been attacked

item.title: Arsenal set to sign former Leeds goalkeeper Meslier
item.dc:creator: Sky Sports Football
item.description: Illan Meslier is set to join Arsenal on a free transfer after being released by Leeds United last week.

item.title: Schengen Area: The politics behind Europe’s visa lottery
item.dc:creator: The Africa Report
item.description: Why is a Senegalese applicant more likely to be refused a European visa than an Ivorian one? Drawing on a cross-analysis of data, our infographics reveal how the Schengen system dispenses trust unevenly, shaping who gets to travel and who is left behind.

item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: For years, the Magnificent Seven tech giants commanded investors’ attention, dominating the S&P 500 Index and determining which way the overall stock market was headed. Those days are over. While the technology-heavy Nasdaq 100 Index is up 16% in 2026 and the S&P 500 has climbed 10%, an inde

item.title: Bill on reduction of maternal mortality scales second reading in Ogun Assembly

item.description: The Speaker of the Ogun State House of Assembly, Oludaisi Elemide, has assured that the proposed Safe Termination of Pregnancy Law would undergo a rigorous process to ensure that its provisions do not conflict with existing laws of the Federal Republic of Nigeria and other extant legislations. item.title: NIN Database Hits 136 Million As Digital Identity Expands
item.dc:creator: The Whistler
item.description: Nigeria’s National Identification Number (NIN) database has reached 136 million registered individuals, reflecting continued expansion of the country’s digital identity programme as the Federal Government intensifies efforts to establish a unified and secure national identity system. The

item.title: I had affairs with married women – Singer Brymo
item.dc:creator: Vanguard

item.description: The Police Service Commission (PSC) has dismissed four senior police officers and sanctioned 31 others for various acts of misconduct.

item.title: Policemen, okada riders in bloody clash in Calabar

item.description: Hundreds of commercial motorcycle riders, popularly known as okada, in the 8 Miles area of Calabar on Tuesday engaged in a bloody clash with the police. item.title: Nigeria’s FX market turnover rises 7.67% to $3.05 billion, highest in three months
item.dc:creator: Nairametrics
item.description: Nigeria's foreign exchange market recorded its strongest weekly turnover of the current quarter, with total transactions in the FX Spot and Derivatives markets rising 7.67% to $3.053 billion in the week ended July 3, 2026, up from $2.835 billion in the week ended June 26, 2026. The incident was contained in a post shared on X on Tuesday by security analyst Baka

item.description: The Senate has raised the alarm over renewed xenophobic attacks on Nigerians in South Africa, threatening that Nigeria might reconsider its diplomatic relations with Pretoria if the violence against its citizens continued. item.description: The Delta State Police Command has demoted an inspector and a corporal who were found guilty of extortion and misconduct following a viral video of them online collecting money from a motorist through a Point-of-Sale terminal inside a police station. The command’s spokesperson, SP Bright Edafe, disc

item.title: Getty’s Lenders Organize on Renewed Risk of ‘Unsustainable’ Debt
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg Markets
item.description: Lenders to Getty Images Holdings Inc. have started to organize after the debt-laden photo archive business scrapped a planned merger with Shutterstock Inc., according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: NASS didn’t allocate budget for fake agency in 2026 budget – spokesman

item.title: We reviewed over 720 petitions after primaries — APC
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: …denies candidates’ substitution National Publicity Secretary of the All Progressives Congress (APC), Felix Morka, has disclosed that the party received and reviewed more than 720 petitions from aggrieved aspirants following the conduct of its primary elections. Speaking on Arise News on Tuesd

item.title: Court nullifies ARCON’s N60bn fine on Facebook Nigeria
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Innocent Anaba A Federal High Court in Lagos has nullified the N60 billion sanction imposed by the Advertising Regulatory Council of Nigeria, ARCON, on Facebook Nigeria Operations Limited over alleged unapproved advertisements targeted at the Nigerian market. The court held that the regulator act

item.title: Reducing Doom Loops with Final Token Preference Optimization
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://www.liquid.ai/blog/antidoom Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48820127 Points: 3 # Comments: 0

item.title: Woman suspected of Monaco bomb attack found dead in Ukraine
item.dc:creator: BBC News World
item.description: Police had been hunting Anastasiia Berezovska after a Ukrainian millionaire was injured in the blast.

item.title: Customs sets N11.07 trillion revenue target for 2026
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: The approval comes after lawmakers previously proposed increasing the agency's 2025 revenue target from N6.584 trillion to as much as N10 trillion, reflecting confidence in Customs' ability to boost Nigeria's non-oil revenue.

item.title: Apple Sales Seen Weathering Hardware Price Increases
item.dc:creator: Yahoo Finance

item.title: Jim's TrueType QR Code Font
item.dc:creator: Hacker News
item.description: Article URL: https://github.com/jimparis/qr-font Comments URL: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48820119 Points: 3 # Comments: 1

item.title: What I would have done if music hadn’t worked out — Davido
item.dc:creator: Tribune
item.description: The award-winning artiste made the disclosure during an interview with PlayKisstra, noting that his passion for entertainment would have led him to own a record label or a media company. The post What I would have done if music hadn’t worked out — Davido appeared first on Tribune Online.

item.title: Senate advances Bill to strengthen Public Complaints Commission
item.dc:creator: Vanguard
item.description: By Henry Umoru ABUJA — A bill seeking to strengthen the Public Complaints Commission (PCC), improve public service delivery and establish a legal framework for the protection of whistleblowers has passed second reading in the Senate. The proposed legislation also seeks to expand the investigative po

item.title: Tinubu promises to replicate FCT’s infrastructure strides across Nigeria
item.dc:creator: PM News
item.description: Wike attributed the successes being recorded in the delivery of infrastructure in FCT to the overwhelming support of Tinubu, particularly the removal of the Administration from the Treasury Single Account.

item.title: Benue APC chairman Omale backs Alia’s second term, says state needs continuity
item.dc:creator: The Sun
item.description: APC Chairman Benjamin Omale says Governor Hyacinth Alia deserves a second term, citing infrastructure, education and agriculture reforms. The post Benue APC chairman Omale backs Alia’s second term, says state needs continuity appeared first on The Sun Nigeria.

item.title: Claude Cowork expands to mobile and web
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch
item.description: With this update, users can start a task from their desk, get status updates on their phone, and pick up the finished output later — even if their laptop is closed.
